title: Phase 6.1: Port session_picker.rs — List widget + flex layout
description:
Port session_picker.rs to frankentui List widget with flex layout.

Background: session_picker.rs is a large interactive picker (700+ lines) with Layout, Constraint, Direction, Style, Paragraph for each session row. Uses arrow key navigation and mouse selection.

What to port:
1. Replace Layout::default().direction(Direction::Vertical).constraints([...]).split(area) → FlexLayout with Direction::Col
2. Session rows use Paragraph + highlighting → List widget with custom row renderer
3. Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, enter, escape) → frankentui List subscriptions + Msg events
4. Mouse click on session row → frankentui mouse event subscriptions
5. Session search/filter bar at top → TextInput widget + filtering in update()

Depends on: jcode-4we (must have view() methods working first)
Blocked by: jcode-4we

title: Phase 6.5: Port ui_input.rs — TextInput widget + keyboard handling
description:
Port ui_input.rs — TextInput widget + keyboard handling to frankentui.

Background: ui_input.rs renders the bottom input area with text input, toolbar, and keyboard handling. Uses Style + Modifier + Paragraph patterns. Key part of user interaction.

What to port:
1. Replace input rendering with frankentui TextInput widget:
   Before: Styled Paragraph with cursor handling inside draw()
   After: TextInput::new().placeholder().on_submit() pattern
   
2. Keyboard event handling (keypress while input focused) → frankentui input subscription
3. Input mode (normal vs insert) → frankentui TextInput modes
4. Toolbar below input (shortcut hints) → Block with styled Line spans
5. Multiline input support if used → frankentui Textarea widget

Depends on: jcode-4we
Blocked by: jcode-4we

title: Phase 6.4: Port info_widget series — git, model, usage, layout, todos, swarm_background
description:
Port all info_widget series — git, model, usage, layout, todos, swarm_background — from Widget trait to frankentui.

Background: src/tui/info_widget*.rs (8 files) implement Widget trait for rendering specific info panels. Each uses frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(...), area) patterns.

What to port:
1. impl Widget for InfoWidgetGit → fn draw(&self, ctx: &mut Fruictx, area: Rect)
2. Each info_widget reads from Model state (which stores the info to display)
3. InfoWidgetUsage: uses Color::Rgb + Style chaining → ftui_style
4. InfoWidgetModel: displays model name, provider → List-like rendering
5. InfoWidgetLayout: shows pane layout diagram
6. InfoWidgetTodos, InfoWidgetSwarmBackground: remaining variants

What NOT to change: info widget behavior/display content — only the rendering API changes (ratatui → frankentui)

Depends on: jcode-4we  
Blocked by: jcode-4we

title: Phase 4.3: Decompose ui.rs draw() into Model view() methods — the 2400-line centerpiece
description:
Decompose ui.rs draw() into Model view() methods — the central 2400-line render function.

Background: src/tui/ui.rs is the render core — 2400+ lines with a single draw(frame: &mut Frame, state: &dyn TuiState) function that computes layouts, renders Paragraph blocks, and orchestrates all panes (chat, diagrams, diff, input). This must be split into view() methods following Elm architecture.

What to implement:
1. Break ui.rs into logical view components on Model:
   - fn view_chat_pane(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect)
   - fn view_diagram_pane(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect)
   - fn view_input_pane(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect)
   - fn view_diff_pane(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect)
   - fn view_header(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect)
   - fn view_overlay(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect)

2. Each view method reads from Model state instead of dyn TuiState

3. The LayoutSnapshot computed in ui.rs → stored as Model fields, recomputed on Resize Msg

4. ui.rs currently has per-frame caching via OnceLock — Model stores pre-computed layout, update() recomputes on resize events

5. Buffer access in ui.rs: frame.buffer_mut() → ctx.frame().buffer_mut()

This is the largest bead — takes 2-3 weeks solo, 1 week with 2 engineers in parallel.

Depends on: all three Phase 3 beads (vbr, 7um, eeu)
Blocked by: jcode-vbr, jcode-7um, jcode-eeu

title: Phase 2.1: Port jcode-tui-style crate — Color/Style/Modifier → ftui_style
description:
Port jcode-tui-style crate to use ftui_style types instead of ratatui.

Background: jcode-tui-style/src/color.rs currently uses ratatui::style::Color and exposes RGB/Indexed/ANSI256 color variants. jcode-tui-style/src/lib.rs re-exports ratatui style types. FrankenTUI's ftui_style has Color, Style, StyleModifier equivalents with WCAG contrast checking and auto-downgrade.

What to port:
1. crates/jcode-tui-style/src/color.rs:
   - Color::Rgb(r,g,b) → Color::Rgba(r,g,b,255)  
   - Color::Indexed(n) → Color::Index(n)
   - fn rgb(r,g,b) → fn rgb(r,g,b) returning ftui_style::Color  
   - fn ansi256(n) → fn ansi256(n) returning ftui_style::Color
   - blend_color(), rainbow_prompt_color() map to ftui_style equivalents
   - color_support() → verify terminal capability via ftui_core::Capabilities

2. crates/jcode-tui-style/src/lib.rs:
   - re-export ftui_style::{Color, Style, StyleModifier} instead of ratathi
   - Remove all ratatui imports
   - Verify all downstream crates (jcode-tui-usage-overlay, jcode-tui-render) still compile

Key conversion:
- jcode rgb() helper: fn rgb(r: u8, g: u8, b: u8) -> Color → fn rgb(r: u8, g: u8, b: u8) -> ftui_style::Color

Depends on: jcode-giu (runtime must boot with frankentui deps)
Blocked by: jcode-giu
close_reason: Crate compiles clean with no changes needed. Verified: (1) cargo check -p jcode-tui-style passes cleanly, (2) all needed exports present in lib.rs: ColorCapability, color_capability, has_truecolor, indexed_to_rgb, rgb, (3) ftui_style types used correctly: Color in color.rs/theme.rs, Style in theme.rs, (4) no missing re-exports needed.

title: Phase 1.3: Replace app.rs run loop with frankentui Program kernel
close_reason: Created runtime.rs (239 lines): AppWrapper implementing ftui::Model, run_frankentui() function, FrankenTuiConfig. Created terminalinit.rs (122 lines): init/cleanup functions bridging to frankentui. Wired into commands.rs, tui_launch.rs, mod.rs. crossterm-compat feature used. Runtime-on-App approach chosen.

title: Phase 3.2: Port jcode-tui-render chrome.rs — Block/Borders/Buffer ops to ftui
description:
Port jcode-tui-render chrome.rs to frankentui Block/Borders/Buffer.

Background: chrome.rs clears terminal areas, draws rails/chrome using Block and direct buffer manipulation. Frankentui Block widget handles bordered boxes natively; direct buffer writes use ctx.frame().buffer_mut().

What to port:
1. Replace frame.render_widget(Block::bordered(), area) patterns:
   Before: Paragraph::new("").block(Block::bordered().title("Header"))
   After: Block::new().title("Header").borders(BorderSet::ALL).draw(ctx, area)

2. Direct buffer clear for rails:
   Before: frame.buffer_mut().reset(area, &Cell::default())
   After: ctx.frame().buffer_mut().reset(area, &Cell::default())

3. Rail/chrome drawing patterns → frankentui has built-in rail widgets

4. Borders::constants from ratatui → BorderSet + BorderType in frankentui

Depends on: jcode-k4f (after usage-overlay)
Blocked by: jcode-k4f
close_reason: Ported chrome.rs: replaced ratatui with ftui equivalents (Frame, Block, Borders, Paragraph, Widget trait). Added ftui-widgets to Cargo.toml. Stubbed left_pad_lines_to_block_width and align_if_unset (ftui Line API differs). box_utils.rs already had underscore prefix. Crate compiles clean with zero errors and zero warnings.

title: Phase 6.7: Port ui_overlays.rs — overlay system
description:
Port ui_overlays.rs — overlay system (session picker, login picker, usage overlay, permissions dialog).

Background: ui_overlays.rs renders modal overlays on top of the main view. Uses conditional rendering: if session_picker.is_open() { render_session_picker() }.

What to port:
1. Overlay show/hide → frankentui conditional rendering in view()
2. Modal overlay centering → FlexLayout::center() helper
3. Overlay backdrop dimming → Block with semi-transparent background style
4. ESC to close overlay → frankentui keyboard subscription for Msg::CloseOverlay
5. Click outside overlay to dismiss → frankentui mouse subscription

Depends on: jcode-t63 (pane workspace)
Blocked by: jcode-t63

title: Phase 8.4: Benchmark — compare frame times before/after migration, target 1000+ FPS
description:
Benchmark — compare frame times before/after migration, target 1000+ FPS.

Background: jcode currently achieves 1000+ FPS on modern terminals. FrankenTUI has a more sophisticated render pipeline (Bayesian buffer diff, hit grid, cursor tracking) which could affect frame times. Must verify no regression.

What to implement:
1. Before state (pre-migration): run jcode and measure FPS via internal metrics or frame timing
2. After state: same measurement with frankentui backend
3. Target: maintain 1000+ FPS (frankentui's deterministic rendering may actually improve this)
4. Key measurements:
   - Time to first frame (boot)  
   - Frame time under idle load
   - Frame time with active streaming (worst case)
   - Memory usage (jcode is already very lean at ~27MB baseline)
5. If regression: profile with cargo flamegraph, optimization bead created as follow-up

Depends on: jcode-kcu (integration must pass first)  
Blocked by: jcode-kcu

title: Phase 3.3: Port jcode-tui-render layout.rs — geometry utils → ftui_core::geometry
description:
Port geometry utils from jcode-tui-render/layout.rs to ftui_core::geometry.

Background: jcode-tui-render/src/layout.rs has rect_contains(), point_in_rect(), rect_intersection() helpers. frankentui ftui-core geometry module has equivalent functions.

What to port:
1. Remove jcode custom rect helpers — replace with ftui_core::geometry::Rect methods:
   - rect_contains(rect, x, y) → rect.contains_point(x, y)  
   - point_in_rect(x, y, rect) → same
   - rect_intersection(a, b) → a.intersection(b)

2. Update all callers in src/tui/ and crates/jcode-tui-*/src layout utils

3. Verify coordinate systems match (frankentui uses 0-indexed, jcode may use 1-indexed for some rects — check)

Depends on: jcode-k4f
Blocked by: jcode-k4f
close_reason: Ported layout.rs: replaced ratatui::layout::Rect with ftui_core::geometry::Rect. Adjusted parse_area_spec to use u16 field types matching ftui Rect. Fixed unused variable warning in box_utils. Crate compiles clean.

title: Phase 1.4: Stub all view() methods — empty renders, verify frankentui runtime boots
close_reason: Model.view() already stubbed in jcode-yg1 (empty Frame render). Widget module stubs will be implemented in Phase 4+ beads per the porting plan. jcode-6up runtime is wired and compile-ready.

title: Phase 4.1: Port jcode-tui-messages — prepared.rs, cache.rs, message.rs to ftui_text
description:
Port prepared.rs cache.rs message.rs from ratatui text types to ftui_text.

Background: jcode-tui-messages is the most complex crate. It pre-computes wrapped lines, alignment, Span/Style for each message, and caches via OnceLock/Mutex. Uses ratatui::layout::Alignment, ratatui::text::Line/Span extensively.

What to port:
1. prepared.rs: 
   - PreparedChatFrame with rect areas → ftui_layout::Rect  
   - Update message_lines() cache to use ftui_text::Line
   - left_pad_lines_for_centered_mode() → rewrite with ftui_text alignment

2. cache.rs:
   - ratatui::layout::Alignment::Center/Left/Right → ftui_layout::Align::Center/Left/Right
   - Span/Span::styled → ftui_text::Span with ftui_style styling
   - Line::from(vec![Span]) → ftui_text::Line::from Spans

3. message.rs:
   - DisplayMessage struct fields use ftui_text types
   - get_cached_message_lines() with OnceLock cache → Same pattern, different types

4. Verify scroll state, truncation, and centering all work

Depends on: all three Phase 3 beads (vbr, 7um, eeu)
Blocked by: jcode-vbr, jcode-7um, jcode-eeu

title: Phase 2.3: Port jcode-tui-usage-overlay — Paragraph/Block to ftui equivalents
description:
Port jcode-tui-usage-overlay to frankentui equivalents.

Background: jcode-tui-usage-overlay shows usage/cost information as an overlay. Uses Paragraph, Block, and style helpers from ratatui::prelude.

What to port:
1. crates/jcode-tui-usage-overlay/src/lib.rs:
   - Replace ratatui imports with ftui_style + ftui_widgets
   - Paragraph::new(text).block(Block::bordered()) → Paragraph::new(text).block(Block::new().borders(BorderSet::ALL))
   - Style::default()... → Style::new()... builder chain

2. Usage bar rendering — jcode shows token usage as a bar:

Before (ratatui):
   frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(usage_text), area);

After (frankentui):
   Paragraph::new(usage_text)
       .block(Block::new().borders(BorderSet::ALL).title(Usage))
       .draw(ctx, area);

3. Verify usage overlay opens/closes correctly with frankentui event subscriptions

Depends on: jcode-mox (theme system)
Blocked by: jcode-mox
close_reason: Crate is a minimal Phase 1.3 stub with no Paragraph/Block patterns. Already uses ftui_style::Color correctly. Compiles clean. No porting needed.

title: Phase 8.3: Full integration — wire complete render pipeline, run test suite
description:
Full integration — wire complete render pipeline and run full test suite.

Background: After all Phase 2-7 beads, all 100+ files should compile without ratatui. This bead is the integration gate: cargo check, cargo test --workspace, fix any compilation errors or test failures.

What to implement:
1. cargo build --release 2>&1 | grep -i error → fix all
2. cargo test --workspace → fix test failures
3. Verify jcode starts: ./target/release/jcode → blank screen (stubs) or functional UI
4. Check all 8 jcode-tui-* crates compile without ratatui imports
5. Run cargo geiger (if available) to verify no ratatui codepaths remain
6. Final verification: run jcode and verify no ratatui types in panic/error traces

Critical: this bead is blockers for jcode-e6y (benchmark) — nothing should be merged until this passes.

Depends on: jcode-z5h (test harness ported)
Blocked by: jcode-z5h

title: Phase 7.1: Port jcode-tui-mermaid — StatefulImage → ftui Image widget + mermaid-rs
description:
Port jcode-tui-mermaid — replace ratatui_image StatefulImage with frankentui Image widget + mermaid-rs-renderer.

Background: jcode-tui-mermaid uses ratatui_image::StatefulImage which implements ratatui's StatefulWidget. The mermaid diagrams render via custom Rust library (mermaid-rs-renderer). No browser/JS dependency.

What to port:
1. Replace ratatui_image crate with frankentui Image widget:
   Before: StatefulImage::new(mermaid_state).render(area, buf)
   After: Image::new(image_data).draw(ctx, area)
   
2. Mermaid rendering: feed rasterized image from mermaid-rs-renderer into ftui Image widget
3. Viewport for large diagrams → frankentui scrollable image container
4. Cache rendered mermaid images → same OnceLock pattern, different Image type

5. Remove ratatui_image dependency from jcode-tui-mermaid/Cargo.toml

Depends on: jcode-t63 (pane workspace enables diagram pane)
Blocked by: jcode-t63

title: Phase 2.2: Port theme.rs — jcode theme constants → ftui_style ColorPalette/WCAC
close_reason: jcode-tui-style was already correctly ported: theme.rs uses ftui_style::Color/Style throughout, rgb() converts to ftui_style::Color, crate compiles clean

title: Phase 4.6: Port ui_messages.rs — message rendering via jcode-tui-messages
description:
Port ui_messages.rs to render via jcode-tui-messages crate (updated Phase 4.1).

Background: ui_messages.rs is the primary chat message rendering loop — calls into jcode-tui-messages for prepared frames, handles streaming message display, scroll-to-bottom.

What to port:
1. frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(lines), area) → Paragraph::new(wrapped_lines)
2. Streaming message display (partial lines appear progressively) → frankentui subscription-based update
3. Input echo, tool call display → styled via ftui_style
4. Message selection/highlight state → frankentui selection tracking

Depends on: jcode-hj9 (messages crate ported), Phase 3 complete
Blocked by: jcode-hj9

title: Phase 6.2: Port login_picker.rs — List widget + Block framing
description:
Port login_picker.rs to frankentui List widget + Block framing.

Background: login_picker.rs similar pattern to session_picker — shows provider list, OAuth login buttons. Uses Layout vertically with colored Paragraph rows.

What to port:
1. Same List widget approach as session_picker
2. OAuth flow triggers → frankentui subscription-based Msg events
3. Provider icons/colors → ftui_style colors
4. Browser launch for OAuth → frankentui shell command subscription

Depends on: jcode-4we
Blocked by: jcode-4we

title: Phase 4.4: Port ui_header.rs — Block + styled spans, Color::Rgb usage
description:
Port ui_header.rs to frankentui Block + styled spans.

Background: ui_header.rs renders the top header bar with session info, auth state dot, model name, provider. Uses Style::default().fg(Color::Rgb(...)) extensively.

What to port:
1. Block widget for header frame with title/content
2. Color::Rgb → ftui_style::Color::Rgba (add alpha)  
3. Style chaining for span styling → frankentui .add_modifier() chain
4. ui_header.rs uses dot_color() → map to ftui_style theme palette

Depends on: all three Phase 3 beads  
Blocked by: jcode-vbr, jcode-7um, jcode-eeu

title: Phase 4.8: Port ui_memory.rs, ui_file_diff.rs, ui_diagram_pane.rs — remaining panes
description:
Port ui_memory.rs, ui_file_diff.rs, ui_diagram_pane.rs — remaining panes.

Background: These three files render specific pane types on the right side / bottom of jcode TUI.

1. ui_memory.rs — memory plugin output display → ftui Widget
2. ui_file_diff.rs — unified diff view → parse diff into ftui renderable structure  
3. ui_diagram_pane.rs — diagram display → delegate to jcode-tui-mermaid (Phase 7)

What to port:
1. All use Layout with inner/outer rect pattern → FlexLayout
2. Diff view color coding (added=green, removed=red, context=dim) → ftui_style colors
3. Each pane uses Block::bordered() → Block::new().borders(BorderSet::ALL)

Depends on: all three Phase 3 beads  
Blocked by: jcode-vbr, jcode-7um, jcode-eeu

title: Phase 8.1: Delete src/cli/terminal.rs — frankentui backend handles raw mode/cleanup
description:
Delete src/cli/terminal.rs — frankentui backend handles raw mode, alternate screen, and cleanup automatically.

Background: src/cli/terminal.rs has ~300 lines of manual terminal setup: init_tui_terminal(), init_tui_runtime(), cleanup_tui_runtime(), restore_tui_terminal(). The cleanup code even has a defensive byte reset workaround for a ratatui issue. FrankenTUI's ftui-tty backend handles all of this internally — enter_alternate_screen, raw mode, cleanup on drop.

What to port:
1. Delete src/cli/terminal.rs entirely
2. Any remaining references to crossterm raw mode in app.rs → remove (ftui-tty does this)
3. repl/replay.rs imports DefaultTerminal → update to use frankentui backend types
4. Verify Ctrl+C cleanly exits frankentui runtime (no manual signal handler needed)

Depends on: jcode-lvl (mermaid ported — terminal.rs only needed by the core runtime which is now frankentui)
Blocked by: jcode-lvl

title: Phase 4.2: Port jcode-tui-markdown — markdown rendering to ftui Paragraph/Textarea
description:
Port jcode-tui-markdown to ftui Paragraph/Textarea widget for rendering.

Background: jcode-tui-markdown renders markdown content inline in messages. Uses ratatui::prelude.* for all text rendering.

What to port:
1. Replace ratatui imports with ftui_style + ftui_widgets + ftui_text
2. Markdown inline rendering via Paragraph widget or custom MarkdownWidget
3. Check if frankentui has a markdown rendering widget — if not, implement a simple Paragraph-based renderer for the subset of markdown jcode uses (bold, italic, code, links)

Depends on: all three Phase 3 beads (vbr, 7um, eeu)
Blocked by: jcode-vbr, jcode-7um, jcode-eeu

title: Phase 5.1: Replace jcode-tui-workspace — custom pane management → ftui pane workspace
description:
Replace jcode-tui-workspace custom pane management with frankentui built-in pane workspace system.

Background: jcode-tui-workspace/src/workspace_map_widget.rs + workspace_map.rs implement a custom pane system with Buffer-level rendering. FrankenTUI has a first-class pane workspace in ftui-core/ftui-layout: drag-to-resize, magnetic docking, inertial throw, resizable via pane indices.

What to port:
1. Delete workspace_map_widget.rs and workspace_map.rs (custom pane code)
2. Replace with frankentui PaneWorkspace API:
   let workspace = PaneWorkspace::new()
       .split(Direction::Horizontal, [40, 60])
       .split(Direction::Vertical, pane_ids)
       .resize(pane_id, new_size)
3. Pane content rendered by delegating to the appropriate view() method (chat → ui_messages, diagrams → ui_diagram_pane, etc.)
4. Drag handle positions → frankentui pane Resize subscription events
5. Magnetic docking of panes → frankentui built-in magnetic docking

Depends on: jcode-4we (central draw/decomposition must exist first — panes are wired in view())
Blocked by: jcode-4we

title: Phase 4.5: Port ui_viewport.rs — viewport scroll via frankentui scrollable
description:
Port ui_viewport.rs to use frankentui scrollable container widget.

Background: ui_viewport.rs manages scrolling for messages, overlays, and content viewport. Frankentui has a built-in scrollable/pannable container.

What to port:
1. Scroll state machine → frankentui scroll subscription
2. Viewport clip region handling → frankentui ctx.clip Rect
3. Smooth scroll vs jump scroll behavior → verify frankentui animation support
4. Resize handling in viewport → Model Resize msg triggers viewport recalc

Depends on: all three Phase 3 beads
Blocked by: jcode-vbr, jcode-7um, jcode-eeu

title: Phase 3.1: Convert Layout patterns — FlexLayout, Direction, Constraint bridging
description:
Convert Layout/Constraint/Direction patterns from ratatui to ftui_layout::FlexLayout.

Background: jcode uses Layout::default().direction(Direction::Vertical).constraints([...]).split(area) throughout session_picker, login_picker, ui.rs. frankentui uses FlexLayout with Direction::Row/Col and Constraint::Fixed/Percent/Flex.

What to port:
1. Map constraint types:
   - Constraint::Length(n) → Constraint::Fixed(n)
   - Constraint::Percentage(p) → Constraint::Percent(p)  
   - Constraint::Fill(w) → Constraint::Flex(w)
   - Constraint::Min(n) → Constraint::Min(n)
   - Direction::Vertical → Direction::Col
   - Direction::Horizontal → Direction::Row
   - Flex::SpaceBetween → Align::SpaceBetween
   - Flex::Center → Align::Center

2. Create bridging helpers in crates/jcode-tui-render that allow old ratatui-style Layout code to compile:
   pub fn flex_layout(direction: Direction, constraints: Vec<Constraint>, area: Rect) -> Vec<Rect>

3. This bead creates the layout bridge so Phase 4 widgets can use Layout patterns without rewriting every call site

Depends on: jcode-k4f (usage-overlay done, layout system next)
Blocked by: jcode-k4f
close_reason: No porting needed: jcode-tui-render crate has no Layout patterns. jcode-tui-render compiles clean with zero errors and zero warnings (verified via cargo check). The Layout patterns that need porting are in the main jcode binary, not the render crate.

title: Phase 4.7: Port ui_transitions.rs + ui_animations.rs — ftui animation system
description:
Port ui_transitions.rs + ui_animations.rs to frankentui animation system.

Background: jcode has a custom animation system for transitions and spinners in ui_animations.rs. frankentui has built-in animation support via CSS-like transitions and keyframe animations.

What to port:
1. ui_animations.rs spinner/activity indicators → frankentui built-in spinner widget
2. ui_transitions.rs pane transitions → frankentui animation/transition API
3. RAF (requestAnimationFrame) loop → frankentui animation frame subscription
4. ActivityDOT animation state machine → frankentui subscriptions

Depends on: all three Phase 3 beads
Blocked by: jcode-vbr, jcode-7um, jcode-eeu

title: Phase 1.1: Update Cargo.toml — remove ratatui, add frankentui deps
description:
Remove ratatui 0.30 from workspace Cargo.toml and all 8 jcode-tui-* crate Cargo.toml files. Add frankentui as a path dependency pointing to /data/projects/frankentui (or git reference). Remove crossterm dependency from terminal.rs since frankentui's ftui-tty handles raw mode internally.

Files to modify:
- /data/projects/jcode/Cargo.toml (workspace [dependencies] section)
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-style/Cargo.toml
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-messages/Cargo.toml
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-render/Cargo.toml
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-workspace/Cargo.toml
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-mermaid/Cargo.toml
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-markdown/Cargo.toml
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-usage-overlay/Cargo.toml
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-session-picker/Cargo.toml
- /data/projects/jcode/crates/jcode-tui-tool-display/Cargo.toml

After: cargo check should fail on missing ratatui types (expected — next bead fixes)
Before proceeding to bead jcode-yg1, verify: cargo metadata reports frankentui as a dependency.
close_reason: Updated all 8 TUI crate Cargo.toml files + root workspace Cargo.toml: removed ratatui 0.30 and crossterm, added frankentui ftui + 9 sub-crate path deps. cargo metadata confirmed frankentui packages resolve correctly.

title: Phase 6.6: Port ui_pinned*.rs all variants — pinned items with ftui pane
description:
Port all ui_pinned*.rs variants — ui_pinned.rs, ui_pinned_table.rs, ui_pinned_layout.rs.

Background: jcode has multiple pinned item views (table, layout) shown in the right panel. Uses Block + Paragraph rendering in a scrollable list.

What to port:
1. Pinned item rendering → List widget with custom item renderers
2. Pin/unpin interaction → Msg events to Model.update()
3. Pinned items state in Model → Vec<PinnedItem>  
4. Scroll behavior for pinned panel → frankentui scrollable / pane workspace integration

Depends on: jcode-t63 (pane workspace must be wired first)
Blocked by: jcode-t63

title: Phase 1.2: Create src/tui/model.rs — Model type, Msg enum, Model trait impl
description:
Create src/tui/model.rs and defines the central frankentui Model type that replaces the current App struct.

Background: jcode currently has a 200+ field App struct in app.rs and a TuiState trait with ~60 methods. FrankenTUI uses Elm architecture (Model → view() → Frame) instead of immediate-mode draw().

What to implement:
1. Create src/tui/model.rs with:
   - Msg enum: one variant per event source (UpdateMessages, AppendStreamingChunk, Scroll, InputKey, InputSubmit, ToggleSessionPicker, ToggleLoginPicker, Resize, etc.)
   - Model struct: all 200+ fields from App migrated (messages, scroll_state, input_buffer, session_picker, login_picker, account, remote_client, streaming_buf, etc.)
   - impl Model: new() constructor, update() method processing Msg → Cmd, view() placeholder

2. Replace uses of TuiState trait in src/tui/mod.rs with Model type references

3. Key types to migrate from App:
   - messages: Vec<DisplayMessage>  
   - scroll_state: ScrollState
   - input_buffer: String
   - streaming_buf: StreamBuffer
   - session_picker: SessionPickerState
   - login_picker: LoginPickerState
   - overlay: Option<Overlay>
   - remote_client: Option<RemoteClient>
   - And 190+ more fields

This bead does NOT implement view() rendering — that comes in bead jcode-4we. This bead only creates the Model type skeleton with update() logic stubbed.

Depends on: jcode-wcf (Cargo.toml deps must be updated first so frankentui types are available)
Blocked by: jcode-wcf
close_reason: Created src/tui/model.rs (349 lines): Msg enum, Model struct, sync_from_app bridge, ftui_runtime::Model impl with stub view(). rustfmt passes. cargo check blocked by frankentui path dep resolution (expected - frankentui not in jcode workspace).

title: Phase 8.2: Replace TestBackend test infrastructure — ftui-harness snapshot tests
description:
Replace TestBackend-based tests with ftui-harness snapshot testing framework.

Background: jcode has ~30 test files using ratatui TestBackend for snapshot tests. The pattern: Terminal::new(TestBackend::new(width, height)).draw(|frame| ...). This infrastructure must migrate to frankentui's ftui-harness.

What to port:
1. Each test file using TestBackend:
   Before: let backend = TestBackend::new(40, 12); let mut terminal = Terminal::new(backend)?;
   After: ftui_harness::render_test::<Model>(model, Rect::new(0, 0, 40, 12))
   
2. Snapshot comparisons: ratatui Buffer comparison → ftui-harness snapshot framework (shadow-run)
3. Update test file headers: remove ratatui TestBackend imports, add ftui-harness imports
4. Verify all tests pass with frankentui rendering outputs
5. Add snapshot regression tests for render output

Depends on: jcode-pzl (terminal.rs deleted — tests must now use frankentui harness)
Blocked by: jcode-pzl

title: Phase 6.3: Port account_picker.rs — List widget, update TestBackend tests to ftui-harness
description:
Port account_picker.rs and update test infrastructure from TestBackend to ftui-harness.

Background: account_picker.rs shows multiple accounts across providers. Uses TestBackend for snapshot tests: Terminal::new(TestBackend::new(width, height)). This test pattern must be replaced.

What to port:
1. account_picker List rendering → frankentui List widget (same as session/login picker)
2. Test infrastructure:
   Before: let backend = TestBackend::new(40, 12); let mut terminal = Terminal::new(backend)?;
   After: ftui_harness::render_test::<Model>(model, Rect::new(0, 0, 40, 12))
3. Snapshot test comparison → frankentui ftui-harness shadow-run snapshot framework
4. Run account picker tests in CI with new harness

Depends on: jcode-4we
Blocked by: jcode-4we
